item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a really fun article to read and I love the concept as a natural extension of the Broken Masquerade Idea where fandom zealots with access to the anomalous try to hack a fictional work to fit their perspective. It's an idea that's long overdue and the crazy escalation is really enjoyable. My one main criticism is that I wish you had fleshed out the webcomic that is being targeted with an artificial plot and characters being created so that we can understand just what is at stake and what is changing, though on the other hand I can understand it being bland and vague to show how this can apply to any piece of fiction. +1 for me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun concept, well-executed narration, and I feel like most all of it of would be accessible to someone with even a glancing knowledge of the dynamics of large, dedicated fandoms in general—I haven't read Homestuck since its conclusion and was never part of the fandom, and it didn't even strike me until reading these posts that this SCP was a direct reference. Easy +1 from me.

(The only downside for me is now I want to give Homestuck a full reread—goodbye to the next couple months, I guess!)

edited to fix typo

item.title: 1 Year Anniversary
item.content:encoded: Happy 4/13 everyone! Hope y'all have been having a good day!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wait does that mean suspenders is in the scp world now?

>B]
>B]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is placed in the future so things get muddled because of that, but I do intend to write a followup to this some day. Don't know when but the ideas are there.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: soullessGamer, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: also if you don't want to update the article i completely understand

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hate to say it, but i think 4413 might be back in keter, given today's events

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really really wanted to hate this, since I hate Homestuck with a burning passion, but sadly, this is a really good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor, but the fact that I didn't pick up on this being a send-up of Homestuck (I've never read it, though I've got a friend who's huge into it) is a good indicator that it's not inaccessible, which I think firmly pushes this into +1 territory. I like SCPs that are funny without being jokes, you know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good call
what did i just read

item.title: Kudos
item.content:encoded:
Amazing!

It reads like the SCP-Object is the social phenomena around the fandom and webcomic, not just the webcomic and fandom respectively. Similar to 1237- it feels incorporeal, and just as viral. (I'm sure there's hella other SCPs that its similar to like this)

I also wondered how the SCP Foundation would deal with the massive infohazard/cognitohazard that is Homestuck and Homestuck fandom

Thank you for answering this unspoken question!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is wonderful.

The premise and the absurdity of the actions taken by the fandoms is enthralling even without any knowledge of the comic at hand. In fact, I didn't even think it was made with a specific comic in mind until I got to the comments.

Maybe it would have been better had I thought of a specific comment, but even without the knowledge, this deserves a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do we have any jazz skips though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just reading this with only vague familiarity with the webcomic, and none with the fandom, fanfic community, and associated drama: this would work much better if the important characters were developed within the skip itself, instead of relying on outside knowledge to provide them. As it is, I enjoy the concept, but it gets boring quickly because it's all about the interaction between characters, but the characters are never developed (within the skip).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good…. if you get the references. As is, this relies WAY too much on outside media, which is only narrowly avoided by the fact that Homestuck isn't mentioned by name. If it were mentioned, I would have to downvote Re: SCPs based on fictional characters. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i dont know what homestuck is about, and im afraid to ask

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, this was the SCP that finally got me to actually sign up for the site properly just so I could +1 and leave a comment. I know that this is at its heart a joke based around the comic in question (and I enjoyed it heartily as such), but I also like this a lot as a kind of commentary on fandom, and the utter viciousness of some fandom wars, as well as the frequently-expressed desire of some fans to save certain characters from their own canons (and/or other fans…).

Also dat 2721 shout-out. Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: giving a +1 because of what the reference but also because i wish i could give points to 2721

Having no knowledge of the specific comic, even basic premises, I feel nothing at all about this. I don't feel like I was ever given a reason to give a shit about this. Without knowing anything, the pacing of this, I think, works against the piece because of a lack of investment.

My .02, this one wasn't aimed at me in the first place, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea what this is referencing, but I do enjoy some meta shenanigans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
anomalous fan communities

yeah, sounds about right

e: meant as independent reply

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a big fan of the premise, though I do get all the references. Upvoted anyway because the writing and overall execution are great.

There's also a small typo in the 22/04/2010 log, it should probably say "the story is taken down" but it says "the story taken down" (didn't want to edit it in case that was deliberate)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the more unique SCP's that I have read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two of the biggest influences on my writing and creative style, put together in one work.

+1 all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
felt like the references werent too overbearing, and it was fun to read!
+1
(also, the parallels between self-insert and metafictional descension are amazing)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently, Homestuck is equivalent to a slur for some people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did you have to censor Homestuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept of a webcomic scp puts me off but this is done alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: absolutely not at all. we have SCP's based directly on media, memes, video games, and all that other jazz.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't there a specific rule about not making SCPs that are veiled references to third-party media, created pretty much expressly for articles like this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with ARD, not terribly interesting, can't really care for it at all. Cared even less when I found out it related to H*mestuck

-1

item.title: Re: You absolute maniac
item.content:encoded: Don't tempt me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can get that. Chances are I'll be writing more metafiction stuff in the same vein as this in the future (followups to Barrels of Prose and Slugs of Pulp are likely), assuming I don't have my interest swayed by other planned projects, so I may return to the concept of a metafictional war in another form at a later point. I'll just have to wait and see how things pan out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can entirely see why you'd feel that way. I'm happy enough with the article as-is so I won't be making adjustments, but I'll be sure to keep this in mind for future stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found myself bored by the halfway point. This is such a high-level overview of the conflict that there's no interesting characters or battles to really talk about — just groups, death tolls, and unoriginal metafictional shenanigans ending with a decidedly anticlimactic bang.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even better, the founding members of that MTF were the two $10,000 tier Kickstarter incentive characters.

item.title: You absolute maniac
item.content:encoded: This is the epilogue we need expansion on. I saw the SCP #, thought “huh this might be Homestuck” based on the date, and slowly got my hopes confirmed. Also, we need stories about The Midnight Crew as part of an MTF now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't say that I like the story and premise, but it's as well written and original as it can be. If it hadn't been this one, a worse one would have taken its place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mobile Task Force Eta-73 ("Trekkie's Tale")

hehe

having fled into a fanfiction of the web serial Worm

hehehehehe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And just like the title, this is something really excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is great but I think it's hamstrung by not detaching itself enough from the piece of media it's very obviously about but refuses to mention by name, which I know is the idea but at the same time "a war waged entirely through combatants self inserting themselves into the narrative" is such a cool concept that it'd be cool to see it explored in a way that's not a homage to a very specific webcomic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely fantastic and deserves so much more attention. It's an intelligent history-book narrative that's both funny and convincing. Very strong +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They recruited the god damn Midnight Crew into a Mobile Task Force. If that's not epic then I don't know what is.

EDIT: I just did an acrobatic fucking pirouette off the handle and onto that upvote button.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: when i die please read this scp at my funeral

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And now, we truly do have the Homestuck SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather dislike the premise of this. Thankfully, only just the premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you know i was gonna make a joke about my upvote based on the style of a certain webcomic but honestly? i feel like continuing to obfuscate it as much as possible is the move. +1.

i cant wait to be a useless piece of shit today and up all these votes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's something i rapidly scrabbled together because I realized today was an opportunity too perfect to pass up.

Image Credit:

  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Xtej1118_xray.jpg

No knowledge of a certain webcomic should be necessary to read this.

now to go pass out.

edit: oh yeah thanks to ch00bakka for crit and kinch for lotsa encouragement. now to sleep for real.
